urgency.message: 5 new CISA KEV addition(s) this week. Patch validation recommended.

items.title: ICO Cautions Healthcare Worker After Princess of Wales Incident
items.summary: Hospital insider escapes criminal prosecution after attempting to sell royal’s medical records

items.title: Oracle Critical Patch Update, June 2026 Security Update Review
items.summary: Oracle released its third quarterly edition of this year’s Critical Patch Update. The update received patches for 245 security vulnerabilities. Some of the vulnerabilities addressed in this update impact more than one product. These patches address vulnerabilities in various product families, includ

items.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
items.summary: Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.

items.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
items.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]

items.title: 5 reasons Microsoft 365 backup isn’t enough for business data protection
items.summary: Microsoft 365 helps keep services running, but protecting and recovering business data remains your responsibility. Acronis breaks down five gaps organizations should consider when evaluating Microsoft 365 data protection. [...]

items.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
items.summary:
Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure.

According to findings from Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon

items.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
items.summary: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]

items.title: Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push
items.summary:
The deal values industrial cybersecurity giant Dragos at $3.25 billion, and runZero and NetRise will operate under Dragos.
The post Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Get Out of Security Debt by Tackling the Exposure Problem
items.summary: Teams digging out of security debt need to answer only two simple questions: Which vulnerabilities in our systems are exposed, and how long should they stay that way?

items.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
items.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]

items.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
items.summary: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]

items.title: No Exploits Required
items.summary:
Four decades of incident response experience suggest that exploits are often the symptom, not the root cause, of today’s cybersecurity failures.
The post No Exploits Required appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Worried About AI Taking Their Jobs Too
items.summary: Analysis of chatter on underground forums by Sophos finds that hackers fear AI could take work away from them

items.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
items.summary: Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]

items.title: Telegram admits it couldn't police exam-leak channels, India tells court
items.summary: India's government has told the Delhi High Court that Telegram was warned about two weeks before it was blocked, and that the platform admitted it could not proactively detect the channels selling leaked exam papers. Telegram says it cooperated and the ban is unlawful. [...]

items.title: The 'vibe coding spectrum' approach to AI-assisted software development
items.summary: Different code deserves different levels of oversight, so calibrate your approach to ‘vibe coding’ accordingly.

items.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
items.summary:
If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it?

For most enterprises, the answer is a simple no.

The rush to adopt internal AI tools has left a massive trail of administrative debt: orphan

items.title: Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation
items.summary:
The Israeli startup provides sovereign AI and cyber defenses for governments and critical infrastructure.
The post Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
items.summary: Cybersecurity company F5 has released out-of-band security updates to address multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical-severity flaws that could allow attackers to execute code on vulnerable systems. [...]

items.title: LATAM Infrastructure Hit by Fortinet and Ivanti Exploits
items.summary: CloudSEK maps Operation Escaneo, a campaign hitting Latin American infrastructure via perimeter bugs

items.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
items.summary:
An independent PCI assessor tested Reflectiz against the new PCI DSS rules. Here is the verdict: See the full QSA assessment here →

When a customer types their card number into your checkout, their browser is running far more than your code. Analytics tags, a tag manager, a support widget, a paymen

items.title: How to Watch the Knicks Parade on NYC Traffic Surveillance Cameras
items.summary: Artist Morry Kolman will be livestreaming feeds of the NBA champions’ ticker-tape parade from NYC’s traffic cameras—and this time, the city’s Department of Transportation isn’t demanding he stop.

items.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies.
The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
items.summary:
The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information.
The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
items.summary:
Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root.
The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Scripting the disassembler: Local agentic reverse engineering through vbdec’s live COM object model
items.summary: Cisco Talos detailed a new approach to reverse engineering that pairs local AI agents with traditional analysis tools like the VB6 disassembler vbdec. Instead of awkwardly bolting AI onto the software, vbdec exposes its parsed data through a live COM interface.

items.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code.
The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary: Richard Horne, the NCSC CEO, said three-quarters of cyber-attacks targeting UK critical infrastructure came from nation-state actors

items.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
items.summary:
Israel-based Entro specializes in non-human identity and credential security solutions, and it will enable SailPoint to enhance its products.
The post SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybercrime Surges in APAC as Digitalization Takes Hold
items.summary: Interpol claims cybercrime accounts for third of crime in over half of Asia and South Pacific countries

items.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
items.summary: "Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.

items.title: The UK Will Scan Asylum-Seekers’ Faces for Age Checks—Despite Knowing the Tech Is Flawed
items.summary: Internal Home Office tests of age-verification technology show the risks of life-altering errors. It’s moving forward anyway.

items.summary: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.

items.summary: CISA’s new directive officially ends federal agencies’ reliance on static vulnerability scores. items.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
items.summary:
An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research.

The threat actor also has at their disposal a dedicated WordPress phishing page that acts as the central hub,

items.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
items.summary: NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.

items.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
items.summary:
Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet.

The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw.

items.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
items.summary: As Kyiv takes steps toward formal accession to the EU, the bloc is integrating Ukraine with its pool of pre-approved cybersecurity incident response companies.
